bound=transformers.PretrainedConfig)\n\n\n@contextlib.contextmanager\ndef config_file_lock(timeout: int = 10):\n    \"\"\"\n    Context manager for file locking when loading pretrained configs.\n\n    This prevents race conditions when multiple processes try to download/load\n    the same model configuration simultaneously.\n\n    Args:\n        timeout: Maximum time to wait for lock acquisition in seconds\n    \"\"\"\n    # Use a single global lock file in HF cache directory\n    # This serializes all model loading operations to prevent race conditions\n    lock_path = Path(HF_MODULES_CACHE) / \"_remote_code.lock\"\n\n    # Create and acquire the lock\n    lock = filelock.FileLock(str(lock_path), timeout=timeout)\n\n    try:\n        with lock:\n            yield\n    except (PermissionError, filelock.Timeout):\n        # Fallback to tempdir\n        tmp_dir = Path(tempfile.gettempdir())\n        tmp_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        tmp_lock_path = tmp_dir / \"_remote_code.lock\"\n        tmp_lock = filelock.FileLock(str(tmp_lock_path), timeout=timeout)\n        try:\n            with tmp_lock:\n                yield\n        except filelock.Timeout:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"failed to acquire tempdir config lock within {timeout} seconds, proceeding without lock\"\n            )\n            # proceed without lock\n            yield\n        except (PermissionError) as e:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"tempdir config lock unavailable due to OS/permission issue: {e}, proceeding without lock\"\n            )\n            # proceed without lock\n            yield\n\n\n@dataclass(kw_only=True)\nclass ModelConfig(Generic[TConfig]):\n    pretrained_config: Optional[TConfig] = None\n    mapping: Mapping = field(default_factory=Mapping)\n\n    # Quantization configs\n    quant_config: QuantConfig = field(default_factory=QuantConfig)\n    # Per linear layer quantization in quant_cfg.json or hf_quant_config.json\n    quant_config_dict: Optional[Dict[str, QuantConfig]] = None\n    # Delay weights creation to DecoderModelForCausalLM.__post_init__\n    # to support mixed quantization.\n    skip_create_weights_in_init: bool = False\n\n    spec_config: Optional[\"DecodingBaseConfig\"] = None\n    lora_config: Optional[\"LoraConfig\"] = None\n    sparse_attention_config: Optional[\"SparseAttentionConfig\"] = None\n\n    is_generation: bool = True\n    max_num_tokens: int = 8192\n    max_seq_len: Optional[int] = None\n\n    moe_max_num_tokens: Optional[int] = None\n    moe_load_balancer: Optional[MoeLoadBalancerConfig] = None\n\n    attn_backend: str = 'TRTLLM'\n    moe_backend: str = 'CUTLASS'  # options can be CUTLASS, TRTLLM\n    # IF true, disables FC2+finalize fusion in CUTLASS MoE backend\n    moe_disable_finalize_fusion: bool = False\n    # If true, use low precision combine in MoE operations (only for NVFP4 quantization)\n    use_low_precision_moe_combine: bool = False\n\n    # NVFP4 GEMM backend configuration - list of backends to consider for auto-selection\n    # Default excludes 'cutedsl' for faster build time. Add 'cutedsl' for extreme perf.\n    nvfp4_gemm_allowed_backends: List[str] = field(\n        default_factory=lambda: ['cutlass', 'cublaslt', 'cuda_core'])\n\n    allreduce_strategy: AllReduceStrategy = AllReduceStrategy.AUTO\n\n    # If true, enable min-latency mode. Currently only used for Llama4.\n    enable_min_latency: bool = False\n\n    # Allow models to select op according to whether CUDA Graphs are used.\n    use_cuda_graph: bool = False\n\n    force_dynamic_quantization: bool = False\n\n    # If true, use torch.compile for embedding layers.\n    enable_torch_compile_for_embedding = False\n\n    extra_attrs: Dict = field(default_factory=dict, repr=False, init=False)\n\n    # cute dsl op configs\n    use_cute_dsl_blockscaling_mm: bool = False\n    use_cute_dsl_blockscaling_bmm: bool = False\n\n    _frozen: bool = field(default=False, init=False, repr=False)\n\n    # If true, ONLY the vision encoder part of the full model is loaded/executed.\n    mm_encoder_only: bool = False\n\n    def __setattr__(self, key, value):\n        \"\"\"\n        Prevent modification of frozen instance attributes.\n        However, we allow modification of 'extra_attrs' attributes for torch.compile\n        and 'pretrained_config' attributes for mutimodal models.\n        'quant_config' is allowed to be modified to set different quantization for VLM.\n        All the other attributes are frozen.\n        This can be bypassed by manually setting '_frozen' to False. The design is\n        to discourage modifying the attributes unintentionally.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self._frozen:\n            if key not in ('_frozen', 'extra_attrs', 'pretrained_config',\n                           'quant_config'):\n                raise AttributeError(\n                    f\"Cannot modify ModelConfig.'{key}' - instance is frozen\")\n        super().__setattr__(key, value)\n\n    def __post_init__(self):\n        if self.pretrained_config and hasattr(self.pretrained_config,\n                                              \"architectures\"):\n            self.is_generation = self.is_generation_model(\n                self.pretrained_config.architectures,\n                mm_encoder_only=self.mm_encoder_only)\n\n        def get_all_reduce_strategy(strategy: str = \"AUTO\"):\n            maps = {\n                \"AUTO\": AllReduceStrategy.AUTO,\n                \"NCCL\": AllReduceStrategy.NCCL,\n                \"UB\": AllReduceStrategy.UB,\n                \"MINLATENCY\": AllReduceStrategy.MIN_LATENCY,\n                \"ONESHOT\": AllReduceStrategy.ONESHOT,\n                \"TWOSHOT\": AllReduceStrategy.TWOSHOT,\n                \"LOWPRECISION\": AllReduceStrategy.LOWPRECISION,\n                \"MNNVL\": AllReduceStrategy.MNNVL,\n                \"NCCL_SYMMETRIC\": AllReduceStrategy.NCCL_SYMMETRIC,\n            }\n            key = strategy.upper()\n            return maps[key] if key in maps else AllReduceStrategy.AUTO\n\n        if isinstance(self.allreduce_strategy, str):\n            self.allreduce_strategy = get_all_reduce_strategy(\n                self.allreduce_strategy)\n\n        # Set default moe_max_num_tokens if not specified\n        # The maximum number of tokens in MoE are multiplied by DP size when attention DP is enabled\n        if self.moe_max_num_tokens is None:\n            self.moe_max_num_tokens = self.max_num_tokens * self.mapping.dp_size\n\n    @property\n    def torch_dtype(self) -> torch.dtype:\n        \"\"\"Get the torch dtype of the model.\"\"\"\n        # TODO: this is an assumption that a HF model is always in bfloat16\n        # We should figure out a better way to handle this if other models\n        # start to not report dtype.\n        return self.pretrained_config.torch_dtype or torch.bfloat16\n\n    @property\n    def fuse_pos_embd(self):\n        if self.attn_backend == 'TRTLLM':\n            return True\n        elif self.attn_backend == 'FLASHINFER':\n            return False\n        return False\n\n    @property\n    def enable_flash_mla(self):\n        if self.attn_backend == 'TRTLLM':\n            if getattr(self.pretrained_config,\n                       \"kv_lora_rank\", None) and getattr(\n                           self.pretrained_config, \"qk_rope_head_dim\", None):\n                head_dim = self.pretrained_config.kv_lora_rank + self.pretrained_config.qk_rope_head_dim\n                if head_dim == 576 and torch.cuda.get_device_capability() == (\n                        9, 0):\n                    return True\n        return False\n\n    def get_quant_config(self, name: Optional[str] = None) -> QuantConfig:\n        if name is None or self.per_layer_quant_configs is None:\n            return self.quant_config\n\n        if name in self.per_layer_quant_configs:\n            return self.per_layer_quant_configs[name]\n\n        raise ValueError(f'quant config of {name} is not found')\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def is_generation_model(model_architectures: Optional[List[str]],\n                            mm_encoder_only: bool = False) -> bool:\n        if model_architectures is None:\n            logger.warning(\n                \"Model architectures is None, default to is_generation_model=True\"\n            )\n            return True\n        if mm_encoder_only:\n            return False\n        return model_architectures[0] not in [\n            \"BertForSequenceClassification\", \"Qwen2ForProcessRewardModel\",\n            \"Qwen2ForRewardModel\", \"LlamaForTextEmbedding\"\n        ]\n        # TODO: should be 'not model_type == ModelType.ENCODER_ONLY'\n        # once ModelType is used in pytorch flow.\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def resolve_moe_backend(moe_backend: str, architecture: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Resolve AUTO moe_backend to a specific backend based on model architecture.\n\n        Args:\n            moe_backend: The configured moe_backend (may be \"AUTO\")\n            architecture: The model architecture name (e.g., \"GptOssForCausalLM\")\n\n        Returns:\n            Resolved backend name (never \"AUTO\")\n        \"\"\"\n        if moe_backend.upper() != \"AUTO\":\n            return moe_backend\n\n        if architecture == \"GptOssForCausalLM\":\n            sm_version = get_sm_version()\n            # Select the best performing backend based on SM version\n            if 100 <= sm_version < 120:  # Blackwell\n                return \"TRTLLM\"\n            elif 90 <= sm_version < 100:  # Hopper\n                return \"TRITON\"\n            else:\n                return \"CUTLASS\"  # Fallback to CUTLASS for other SM versions (e.g., SM120)\n\n        return \"CUTLASS\"\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def load_modelopt_quant_config(quant_config_file, checkpoint_dir,\n                                   moe_backend):\n        quant_config = QuantConfig()\n        layer_quant_config = None\n\n        with open(quant_config_file) as f:\n            quant_config_dict = json.load(f)\n\n        json_quant_configs = quant_config_dict['quantization']\n\n        quant_config.quant_algo = json_quant_configs.get('quant_algo', None)\n        # fp8_pb_wo from modelopt is the same as FP8_BLOCK_SCALES\n        if quant_config.quant_algo == \"fp8_pb_wo\":\n            quant_config.quant_algo = 'FP8_BLOCK_SCALES'\n        quant_config.kv_cache_quant_algo = json_quant_configs.get(\n            'kv_cache_quant_algo', None)\n        quant_config.group_size = json_quant_configs.get('group_size', None)\n        quant_config.exclude_modules = json_quant_configs.get(\n            'exclude_modules', None)\n\n        if quant_config.quant_algo == QuantAlgo.MIXED_PRECISION:\n            json_extended_quant_configs: dict = {}\n            # See tests/unittest/llmapi/test_llm_quant.py\n            try:\n                mixed_quant_config_file = transformers.utils.hub.cached_file(\n                    checkpoint_dir, 'quant_cfg.json')\n                with open(mixed_quant_config_file) as fm:\n                    json_extended_quant_configs = json.load(fm)\n            except Exception:\n                logger.info(\n                    f\"No quant_cfg.json found for layer quant info, using hf_quant_config.json.\"\n                )\n            json_quant_configs.update(json_extended_quant_configs)\n            # kv_cache_quant_algo is global regardless of MIXED_PRECISION\n            kv_cache_quant_algo = json_quant_configs.get(\n                'kv_cache_quant_algo', None)\n            mixed_quant_configs = json_quant_configs.get(\n                'quantized_layers', None)\n            if (kv_quant_lhs := json_extended_quant_configs.get(\n                    \"kv_cache_quant_algo\", None)) is not None and (\n                        kv_quant_rhs :=\n                        quant_config.kv_cache_quant_algo) is not None:\n                if kv_quant_lhs != kv_quant_rhs:\n                    raise RuntimeError(\n                        f\"The kvcache config in 'quant_cfg.json', {kv_quant_lhs},\"\n                        f\"is different from 'hf_quant_config.json', {kv_quant_rhs}!\"\n                    )\n            quant_config.kv_cache_quant_algo = json_quant_configs[\n                \"kv_cache_quant_algo\"]\n            for layer in mixed_quant_configs:\n                config = QuantConfig()\n                config.kv_cache_quant_algo = kv_cache_quant_algo\n                config.quant_algo = mixed_quant_configs[layer]['quant_algo']\n                config.group_size = mixed_quant_configs[layer].get(\n                    'group_size', None)\n                mixed_quant_configs[layer] = config\n            layer_quant_config = mixed_quant_configs\n        elif quant_config.quant_algo == QuantAlgo.FP8_BLOCK_SCALES:\n            if quant_config.group_size is None:\n                quant_config.group_size = 128\n\n        if moe_backend == 'TRTLLM' and quant_config.quant_algo == \"FP8_BLOCK_SCALES\" and quant_config.exclude_modules is None:\n            quant_config.exclude_modules = [\n                \"*kv_b_proj*\", \"*k_b_proj*\", \"*eh_proj\"\n            ]\n        return quant_config, layer_quant_config\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def get_mxfp4_quant_algo(moe_backend, is_dynamic_quant=False):\n        quant_algo = ModelConfig.override_quant_algo()\n        if quant_algo is None and not is_dynamic_quant:\n            if get_sm_version() >= 100:\n                if moe_backend == 'TRITON':\n                    return QuantAlgo.W4A8_MXFP4_FP8\n                else:\n                    return QuantAlgo.W4A8_MXFP4_MXFP8\n            else:\n                return QuantAlgo.W4A16_MXFP4\n        else:\n            return quant_algo\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def load_hf_quant_config(hf_quant_config, moe_backend):\n        quant_config = QuantConfig()\n        layer_quant_config = None\n\n        # Read exclude_modules from HF config if present (HF format module names)\n        hf_exclude_modules = hf_quant_config.get('modules_to_not_convert', None)\n\n        # DeepSeek V3 FP8 ckpt\n        if hf_quant_config.get(\"quant_method\") == \"fp8\" and hf_quant_config.get(\n                \"weight_block_size\", []):\n            quant_config.quant_algo = QuantAlgo.FP8_BLOCK_SCALES\n\n            block_size = hf_quant_config.get(\"weight_block_size\", [])\n            assert tuple(block_size) == (\n                128, 128), \"FP8_BLOCK_SCALES only supports block_size=(128,128)\"\n            quant_config.group_size = block_size[0]\n\n            # Set default exclude_modules for FP8_BLOCK_SCALES\n            if moe_backend == 'TRTLLM':\n                default_exclude = [\"*kv_b_proj*\", \"*k_b_proj*\", \"*eh_proj\"]\n            else:\n                default_exclude = [\"*eh_proj\"]\n\n            # Merge HF config's modules_to_not_convert with default exclude_modules\n            if hf_exclude_modules is not None:\n                quant_config.exclude_modules = list(\n                    set(hf_exclude_modules + default_exclude))\n            else:\n                quant_config.exclude_modules = default_exclude\n        # MXFP4 checkpoints.\n        elif hf_quant_config.get(\"quant_method\") == \"mxfp4\":\n            quant_config.quant_algo = ModelConfig.get_mxfp4_quant_algo(\n                moe_backend)\n            quant_config.group_size = 32\n\n            # Default exclude_modules for MXFP4 (TRTLLM internal format)\n            default_exclude = [\n                'block.*.attn.out', 'block.*.mlp.gate', 'block.*.attn.qkv',\n                'embedding', 'unembedding'\n            ]\n\n            # Merge HF config's modules_to_not_convert with default exclude_modules\n            if hf_exclude_modules is not None:\n                quant_config.exclude_modules = list(\n                    set(hf_exclude_modules + default_exclude))\n            else:\n                quant_config.exclude_modules = default_exclude\n\n        # NOTE: This is for llm-compressor's quantized checkpoints.\n        elif hf_quant_config.get(\"quant_method\") == \"compressed-tensors\":\n            config_groups = hf_quant_config.get(\"config_groups\")\n            if config_groups is None:\n                raise ValueError(\n                    f\"config_groups is not set in {hf_quant_config}.\")\n\n            weights_quant_config = config_groups[\"group_0\"][\"weights\"]\n            inputs_quant_config = config_groups[\"group_0\"][\"input_activations\"]\n            weights_quant_strategy = weights_quant_config[\"strategy\"]\n            inputs_quant_strategy = inputs_quant_config[\"strategy\"]\n\n            if weights_quant_config[\"num_bits\"] == 8:\n                if weights_quant_strategy == \"channel\":\n                    if inputs_quant_strategy != \"token\":\n                        raise ValueError(\n                            f\"Unsupported inputs_quant_strategy: {inputs_quant_strategy}.\"\n                        )\n                    quant_config.quant_algo = QuantAlgo.FP8_PER_CHANNEL_PER_TOKEN\n                elif weights_quant_strategy == \"block\":\n                    if inputs_quant_strategy != \"group\":\n                        raise ValueError(\n                            f\"Unsupported inputs_quant_strategy: {inputs_quant_strategy}.\"\n                        )\n                    quant_config.quant_algo = QuantAlgo.FP8_BLOCK_SCALES\n                    group_size = inputs_quant_config[\"group_size\"]\n\n                    # NOTE: TRT-LLM only supports group_size=128 for FP8_BLOCK_SCALES.\n                    if group_size != 128:\n                        raise ValueError(\n                            f\"Unsupported group_size: {group_size}. Supported: 128.\"\n                        )\n                    quant_config.group_size = group_size\n\n                else:\n                    raise ValueError(\n                        f\"Unsupported weights_quant_strategy: {weights_quant_strategy}. \"\n                        \"Supported strategies: 'channel', 'block'.\")\n            else:\n                raise ValueError(\n                    f\"Unsupported quant_bits: {weights_quant_config['num_bits']}. \"\n                    \"Supported: 8.\")\n\n            quant_config.exclude_modules = hf_quant_config.get(\"ignore\", [])\n        return quant_config, layer_quant_config\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def load_quant_config_from_dtypes_json(dtypes_json_file, moe_backend: str):\n        quant_config = QuantConfig()\n        layer_quant_config = None\n\n        exclude_modules = set()\n        has_mxfp4 = False\n        is_dynamic_quant = False\n        with open(dtypes_json_file) as f:\n            dtypes_json = json.load(f)\n            for layer, dtype in dtypes_json.items():\n                if layer.endswith(\"weight\"):\n                    if dtype == \"BF16\" or dtype == \"FP16\":\n                        names = layer.split(\".\")\n                        exclude_modules.add('.'.join(names[:-1]))\n                    elif dtype == \"MXFP4\":\n                        # This is the path for the fp8 checkpoint which requires dynamic quantization.\n                        is_dynamic_quant = True\n                        has_mxfp4 = True\n                elif layer.endswith(\"weight.blocks\"):\n                    scale_name = layer.replace(\"weight.blocks\", \"weight.scales\")\n                    scale_dtype = dtypes_json.get(scale_name, None)\n                    assert scale_dtype == \"UE8\"\n                    is_dynamic_quant = False\n                    has_mxfp4 = True\n\n        if has_mxfp4:\n            quant_config.quant_algo = ModelConfig.get_mxfp4_quant_algo(\n                moe_backend, is_dynamic_quant)\n            quant_config.group_size = 32\n            quant_config.exclude_modules = list(exclude_modules)\n            logger.info(f\"Setting quant_config: {quant_config}\")\n\n        return quant_config, layer_quant_config\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def override_quant_algo():\n        new_algo = os.environ.get(\"OVERRIDE_QUANT_ALGO\", None)\n        supported_algos = {\n            \"W4A16_MXFP4\": QuantAlgo.W4A16_MXFP4,\n            \"W4A8_MXFP4_MXFP8\": QuantAlgo.W4A8_MXFP4_MXFP8,\n            \"W4A8_MXFP4_FP8\": QuantAlgo.W4A8_MXFP4_FP8,\n        }\n        if new_algo is not None:\n            if new_algo.upper() in supported_algos:\n                return supported_algos[new_algo.upper()]\n            else:\n                logger.warning(\n                    f\"Unsupported quant algo: {new_algo}, supported algos: {supported_algos.keys()}\"\n                )\n        return None\n\n    @classmethod\n    def from_pretrained(cls,\n                        checkpoint_dir: str,\n                        trust_remote_code=False,\n                        **kwargs):\n        # Use file lock to prevent race conditions when multiple processes\n        # try to import/cache the same remote model config file\n        with config_file_lock():\n            # When handling the case where model_format is TLLM_ENGINE\n            # send cyclic requests to the NONE URL.\n            if checkpoint_dir is not None:\n                pretrained_config = load_pretrained_config(\n                    checkpoint_dir,\n                    trust_remote_code=trust_remote_code,\n                    **kwargs,\n                )\n                if pretrained_config.architectures[\n                        0] == \"DeepseekV32ForCausalLM\":\n                    sparse_attention_config = kwargs.get(\n                        'sparse_attention_config')\n                    if sparse_attention_config:\n                        index_n_heads = sparse_attention_config.index_n_heads or pretrained_config.index_n_heads\n                        index_head_dim = sparse_attention_config.index_head_dim or pretrained_config.index_head_dim\n                        index_topk = sparse_attention_config.index_topk or pretrained_config.index_topk\n                        indexer_max_chunk_size = sparse_attention_config.indexer_max_chunk_size\n                        skip_indexer_for_short_seqs = sparse_attention_config.skip_indexer_for_short_seqs\n                    else:\n                        index_n_heads = pretrained_config.index_n_heads\n                        index_head_dim = pretrained_config.index_head_dim\n                        index_topk = pretrained_config.index_topk\n                        indexer_max_chunk_size = None\n                        skip_indexer_for_short_seqs = True\n                    kwargs[\n                        'sparse_attention_config'] = DeepSeekSparseAttentionConfig(\n                            index_n_heads=index_n_heads,\n                            index_head_dim=index_head_dim,\n                            index_topk=index_topk,\n                            indexer_max_chunk_size=indexer_max_chunk_size,\n                            skip_indexer_for_short_seqs=\n                            skip_indexer_for_short_seqs)\n            else:\n                raise ValueError(\n                    \"checkpoint_dir is None. Cannot load model config without a valid checkpoint directory.\"\n                )\n\n        # Get cached file from path or repo id, return None if not exists.\n        def cached_file(path_or_repo_id, file_name):\n            try:\n                return transformers.utils.hub.cached_file(\n                    path_or_repo_id, file_name)\n            except OSError:\n                return None\n\n        # Some checkpoints lack torch_dtype, populate with dtype\n        pretrained_config.torch_dtype = getattr(pretrained_config, 'dtype',\n                                                None)\n\n        # Apply model_kwargs to override config parameters if provided\n        model_kwargs = kwargs.pop('model_kwargs', None)\n        if model_kwargs:\n\n            def _recursive_update_config(config: transformers.PretrainedConfig,\n                                         update_dict: Dict[str, Any]):\n                \"\"\"\n                Recursively update a PretrainedConfig object with values from update_dict.\n                Args:\n                    config: PretrainedConfig object to update\n                    update_dict: Dictionary with values to update in the config\n                \"\"\"\n                for key, value_new in update_dict.items():\n                    target_value = getattr(config, key, None)\n\n                    # Handle nested PretrainedConfig objects when value is a dict\n                    if isinstance(value_new, dict) and isinstance(\n                            target_value, transformers.PretrainedConfig):\n                        # Recursively update the nested config\n                        logger.info(\n                            f\"Recursively updating nested config: {key}\")\n                        _recursive_update_config(target_value, value_new)\n                    elif (key in [\"torch_dtype\", \"dtype\"]\n                          and isinstance(value_new, str)\n                          and value_new != \"auto\"):\n                        # check special handling of torch_dtype (DEPRECATED!) and dtype keys to ensure we\n                        # use the correct torch.dtype object instead of a string.\n                        dtype = getattr(torch, value_new)\n                        assert isinstance(dtype,\n                                          torch.dtype), f\"Invalid {dtype=}\"\n                        setattr(config, key, dtype)\n                        logger.info(\n                            f\"Applied model_kwargs: {key}={dtype} (previous value: {target_value})\"\n                        )\n                    else:\n                        # Direct update for simple values\n                        setattr(config, key, value_new)\n                        logger.info(\n                            f\"Applied model_kwargs: {key}={value_new} (previous value: {target_value})\"\n                        )\n\n            _recursive_update_config(pretrained_config, model_kwargs)\n\n        quant_config = QuantConfig()\n        layer_quant_config = None\n        moe_backend = kwargs.get('moe_backend', 'AUTO')\n        # Resolve AUTO to specific backend based on model architecture\n        architecture = pretrained_config.architectures[\n            0] if pretrained_config.architectures else \"\"\n        moe_backend = cls.resolve_moe_backend(moe_backend, architecture)\n        kwargs['moe_backend'] = moe_backend\n\n        # quantized ckpt in modelopt format\n        if quant_config_file := cached_file(checkpoint_dir,\n                                            'hf_quant_config.json'):\n            quant_config, layer_quant_config = cls.load_modelopt_quant_config(\n                quant_config_file, checkpoint_dir, moe_backend)\n        # quantized ckpt in other formats\n        elif hasattr(pretrained_config, \"quantization_config\"):\n            hf_quant_config = pretrained_config.quantization_config\n            quant_config, layer_quant_config = cls.load_hf_quant_config(\n                hf_quant_config, moe_backend)\n        elif quant_config_file := cached_file(checkpoint_dir, 'dtypes.json'):\n            quant_config, layer_quant_config = cls.load_quant_config_from_dtypes_json(\n                quant_config_file, moe_backend)\n\n        model_config = cls(pretrained_config=pretrained_config,\n                           quant_config=quant_config,\n                           quant_config_dict=layer_quant_config,\n                           **kwargs)\n        model_config._frozen = True\n        return model_config\n\n    def get_bindings_model_config(self,\n                                  tokens_per_block: Optional[int] = None\n                                  ) -> \"ModelConfigCpp\":\n        \"\"\"\n        This method is used to construct the bindings config for the model.\n        Currently it adheres to gptJsonConfig.cpp::createModelConfig, which assumes\n        that an engine has been created.\n\n        Args:\n            tokens_per_block: The number of tokens per block. Please note that in PyTorch flow tokens_per_block is not available in the model config, instead it is defined in the executor config.\n\n        Returns:\n            The bindings model config.\n        \"\"\"\n        # TODO smor- this isn't robust, and currently tested for LlamaConfig only\n        # TODO smor- currently assuming no rnn layers, no MOE\n        from tensorrt_llm.bindings import ModelConfig as ModelConfigCpp\n\n        # Attention DP should not shard attention heads; use attn_tp_size=1 in that case\n        # so downstream KV calculations see the full (non-partitioned) head count.\n        attn_tp_size = self.mapping.attn_tp_size if not self.mapping.enable_attention_dp else 1\n        attn_cp_size = self.mapping.attn_cp_size\n\n        num_heads = self.pretrained_config.num_attention_heads // (\n            attn_tp_size * attn_cp_size)\n\n        hidden_size = self.pretrained_config.hidden_size // attn_tp_size\n        num_layers = self.pretrained_config.num_hidden_layers\n        num_attention_layers = self.get_num_attention_layers()\n        if (self.spec_config is not None\n                and self.spec_config.spec_dec_mode.is_mtp_one_model()):\n            num_layers += self.spec_config.num_nextn_predict_layers\n            num_attention_layers += self.spec_config.num_nextn_predict_layers\n\n        model_config_cpp = ModelConfigCpp(\n            vocab_size=self.pretrained_config.vocab_size,\n            num_layers=num_layers,\n            num_attention_layers=num_attention_layers,\n            num_rnn_layers=0,\n            num_heads=num_heads,\n            hidden_size=hidden_size,\n            data_type=torch_dtype_to_binding(\n                self.pretrained_config.torch_dtype))\n\n        # For kv cache size calculation: set tokens_per_block\n        if tokens_per_block is None:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"tokens_per_block is not set, using default value {model_config_cpp.tokens_per_block}\"\n            )\n        else:\n            model_config_cpp.tokens_per_block = tokens_per_block\n\n        num_key_value_heads = getattr(self.pretrained_config,\n                                      \"num_key_value_heads\", num_heads)\n        if isinstance(num_key_value_heads, (list, tuple)):\n            # Per-layer KV heads (e.g., Nemotron-NAS, variable GQA models)\n            num_kv_heads_per_layer = [\n                kv_heads // (attn_tp_size * attn_cp_size)\n                for kv_heads in num_key_value_heads\n            ]\n            model_config_cpp.num_kv_heads_per_layer = num_kv_heads_per_layer\n        else:\n            num_kv_heads = num_key_value_heads // (attn_tp_size * attn_cp_size)\n            model_config_cpp.set_num_kv_heads(num_kv_heads)\n\n        mlp_hidden_size = None\n        if self.pretrained_config.intermediate_size is not None:\n            mlp_hidden_size = self.pretrained_config.intermediate_size // self.mapping.tp_size\n        else:\n            # TODO: once tensorrt_llm._torch.AutoConfig is implemented, the following logic\n            # should be moved to tensorrt_llm._torch.AutoConfig of the relevant modeling_xxx file\n            if hasattr(self.pretrained_config, \"architectures\"\n                       ) and self.pretrained_config.architectures is not None:\n                architectures = self.pretrained_config.architectures\n                if len(architectures\n                       ) == 1 and architectures[0] == \"DeciLMForCausalLM\":\n                    mlp_hidden_size = self._infer_nemotron_ffn_mult(\n                    ) // self.mapping.tp_size\n                else:\n                    raise ValueError(\n                        f\"Inferring mlp hidden size for model architecture: {architectures} isn't supported yet\"\n                    )\n        if mlp_hidden_size is None:\n            raise ValueError(\n                f\"Failed to infer mlp hidden size for model: {self.pretrained_config.model_type}\"\n            )\n\n        # For kv cache size calculation: set size_per_head\n        head_dim_names = [\"head_size\", \"head_dim\"]\n        head_size = None\n        for head_dim_name in head_dim_names:\n            if hasattr(self.pretrained_config, head_dim_name):\n                value = getattr(self.pretrained_config, head_dim_name)\n                if value is not None:\n                    head_size = value\n                    break\n\n        if head_size is None:\n            assert hidden_size % num_heads == 0, (\n                f\"hidden_size ({hidden_size}) must be divisible by num_heads ({num_heads})\"\n            )\n            calculated_head_size = hidden_size // num_heads\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"head_size/head_dim is not set or None, using default value {calculated_head_size}\"\n            )\n            head_size = calculated_head_size\n\n        model_config_cpp.mlp_hidden_size = mlp_hidden_size\n        model_config_cpp.size_per_head = head_size\n\n        # NOTE: this method is not robust, for Gemma3ForCausalLM only\n        layer_types = self.get_layer_types()\n        if layer_types is not None:\n            model_config_cpp.layer_types = layer_types\n\n        return model_config_cpp\n\n    def _infer_nemotron_ffn_mult(self):\n        # TODO smor: this is a hack to support Nemotron-Super-49B-v1 with LoRA, tracked by TRTLLM-5045 ticket\n        # Nemotron-NAS has variable ffn_mult for each layer, we need to find the maximum\n        # so that we don't set a too small mlp_hidden_size. This solution leads to a memory\n        # consumption that is higher than required.\n        biggest_ffn_mult = max([\n            (x.ffn.ffn_mult if x.ffn.ffn_mult is not None else 0)\n            for x in self.pretrained_config.block_configs\n        ])\n\n        from tensorrt_llm._torch.models.modeling_nemotron_nas import \\\n            _ffn_mult_to_intermediate_size\n        mlp_hidden_size = _ffn_mult_to_intermediate_size(\n            biggest_ffn_mult, self.pretrained_config.hidden_size)\n\n        return mlp_hidden_size\n\n    def get_layer_types(self) -> Optional[List[LayerTypeCpp]]:\n        \"\"\"\n        This method is a hack to support the effort to switch to KvCacheManagerCpp.\n        Currently, it is only tested for Gemma3ForCausalLM. For other models, it will return None.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.pretrained_config.architectures[0] in [\"Gemma3ForCausalLM\"]:\n            logger.debug(\n                f\"Setting layer types for {self.pretrained_config.architectures}\"\n            )\n            return [\n                LayerTypeCpp.ATTENTION,\n            ] * self.pretrained_config.num_hidden_layers\n        else:\n            return None\n\n    def get_num_attention_layers(self):\n        if is_nemotron_hybrid(self.pretrained_config):\n            return self.pretrained_config.hybrid_override_pattern.count(\"*\")\n        elif hasattr(\n                self.pretrained_config, \"architectures\"\n        ) and self.pretrained_config.architectures is not None and self.pretrained_config.architectures[\n                0] in [\"Qwen3NextForCausalLM\"]:\n            # Qwen3NextForCausalLM has hybrid attention pattern(1:3 full attention:linear attention),\n            # we need to calculate the number of fullattention layers\n            return self.pretrained_config.num_hidden_layers // self.pretrained_config.full_attention_interval\n        else:\n            return self.pretrained_config.num_hidden_layers\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":478,"oldLines":6,"newStart":478,"newLines":7,"lines":["             \"W4A16_MXFP4\": QuantAlgo.W4A16_MXFP4,","             \"W4A8_MXFP4_MXFP8\": QuantAlgo.W4A8_MXFP4_MXFP8,","             \"W4A8_MXFP4_FP8\": QuantAlgo.W4A8_MXFP4_FP8,","+            \"W4A8_MXFP4_INT8\": QuantAlgo.W4A8_MXFP4_INT8,","         }","         if new_algo is not None:","             if new_algo.upper() in supported_algos:"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

Optional[TensorParallelMode] = None,\n    device: torch.device = torch.device('cpu'),\n    return_slice_indices: bool = False,\n) -> torch.Tensor:\n    # Skip device transfers on integrated GPUs to conserve shared memory\n    if weight.device.type != device.type and is_device_integrated():\n        # For integrated GPU systems (e.g., DGX Spark), CPU and GPU share limited physical memory.\n        # Avoiding device transfers reduces memory consumption and unnecessary data copies,\n        # enabling support for larger models on memory-constrained systems.\n        logger.warning_once(\n            f\"[load_weight_shard] Skipping device transfer from {weight.device} to {device} on integrated GPU to conserve shared memory.\",\n            key=\"load_weight_shard_skip_device_transfer_with_integrated_gpu\")\n        device = weight.device\n    if isinstance(weight, torch.Tensor):\n        tensor_shape = weight.shape\n\n        def result if return_slice_indices else result[0]\n\n    # WAR to check whether it is a safetensor slice since safetensor didn't register the type to the module\n    # safetensors slice, supports lazy tensor_parallel_mode is None or tensor_parallel_size <= 1:\n        return maybe_convert_to_torch_tensor(weight)\n\n    split_dim = TensorParallelMode.split_dim(tensor_parallel_mode)\n\n    if len(tensor_shape) == 1 and split_dim == 1:\n        return maybe_convert_to_torch_tensor(weight)\n\n    width = tensor_shape[split_dim]\n    if width == 1:\n        return dtype and weight_id for weight only quantization mode.\n\n    Returns:\n        tuple[torch.dtype, int]: (weight_dtype, weight_id) where:\n            - weight_dtype: torch.int8 for INT8 weights, torch.quint4x2 for INT4 weights\n            - weight_id: 1 for INT8, 2 for INT4 (used for weight packing)\n    \"\"\"\n    assert module.quant_config is not None and module.quant_config.layer_quant_mode.is_weight_only(\n    ), \"This function should only be called when the module has weight-only quantization enabled.\"\n\n    if else:\n        raise ValueError(\n            f\"Unsupported quant_mode: {module.quant_config.layer_quant_mode}\")\n\n\nclass LinearMethodBase(ABC):\n    \"\"\"\n    Base class for all linear methods.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    def create_weights(self, module: Linear, in_features: int,\n                       out_features: int, bias: bool, dtype: torch.dtype, *args,\n                       **kwargs):\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    def apply(self, module: Linear, input: torch.Tensor,\n              bias: Optional[torch.Tensor], *args,                               tp_group: List[int], *args, **kwargs):\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def load_weights(self,\n                     module: Linear,\n                     weights: List[Dict],\n                     weight_mode: WeightMode,\n                     allow_partial_loading: bool = False):\n        \"\"\"\n        Load weights from the checkpoint.\n        \"\"\"\n        kargs = {}\n        if isinstance(self, UnquantizedLinearMethod):\n            kargs['allow_partial_loading'] = allow_partial_loading\n        if weight_mode == WeightMode.VANILLA:\n process_weights_after_loading_vanilla(self, module: Linear):\n        \"\"\"\n        Process quantization weights and scales after loading weights for vanilla linear layer.\n        \"\"\"\n\n    def process_weights_after_loading_fused_qkv_linear(self, module: Linear):\n        \"\"\"\n        Process quantization weights and scales after loading weights for fused QKV linear layer.\n        \"\"\"\n\n    def process_weights_after_loading_fused_gate_up_linear(\n            self, module: Linear):\n        \"\"\"\n        Process quantization weights and scales after loading weights for fused gate up linear layer.\n        \"\"\"\n\n\nclass UnquantizedLinearMethod(LinearMethodBase):\n\n    def create_weights(self, module: Linear, in_features: int,\n                       out_features: int, bias: bool, dtype: torch.dtype):\n        weight_shape = (out_features, in_features)\n        module.weight = Parameter(torch.empty(weight_shape, dtype=dtype),\n                                  requires_grad=False)\n\n        if bias:\n            module.bias = Parameter(torch.empty((out_features), dtype=dtype),\n                                    requires_grad=False)\n        else:\n            module.register_parameter(\"bias\", None)\n\n        module.rebuild_tensor_metadata = {}\n\n    def apply(self, module: Linear, input: torch.Tensor,\n              bias: Optional[torch.Tensor]):\n        if module.use_custom_cublas_mm:\n            output = torch.ops.trtllm.cublas_mm(input,\n                                                module.weight.t(),\n                                                bias,\n                                                out_dtype=None)\n        else:\n            output = F.linear(input, module.weight, bias)\n        return = False) -> None:\n        load_weights_vanilla_helper(module,\n                                    weights,\n                                    allow_partial_loading=allow_partial_loading)\n\n    def load_weights_fused_qkv_linear(\n            self,\n            module: Linear,\n            weights: List[Dict],\n            allow_partial_loading: bool = False) -> None:\n        q_weight, k_weight, v_weight = load_weights_fused_qkv_helper(\n            module, weights, allow_partial_loading=allow_partial_loading)\n        if not allow_partial_loading:\n            copy_weight(module.weight, torch.cat(\n                (q_weight, k_weight, v_weight)))\n        else:\n            for shard_key, weight in zip(('q', 'k', 'v'),\n                                         (q_weight, k_weight, v_weight)):\n                if weight is not None:\n                    assert shard_key in module.fused_weight_shard_indices_mapping, f\"Shard key {shard_key} not found in fused weight shard indices mapping\"\n                    shard_offset, shard_size = module.fused_weight_shard_indices_mapping[\n                        shard_key]\n                    copy_weight_shard(module.weight, weight, shard_offset,\n                                      shard_size)\n\n    def load_weights_fused_gate_up_linear(\n            self,\n            module: Linear,\n            weights: List[Dict],\n            allow_partial_loading: bool = False) -> None:\n        gate_weight, up_weight = load_weights_fused_gate_up_helper(\n            module, weights, allow_partial_loading=allow_partial_loading)\n        if not allow_partial_loading:\n            copy_weight(module.weight, torch.cat((gate_weight, up_weight)))\n        else:\n            for shard_key, weight in zip(('gate', 'up'),\n                                         (gate_weight, up_weight)):\n                if weight is not None:\n                    assert shard_key in module.fused_weight_shard_indices_mapping, f\"Shard key {shard_key} not found in fused weight shard indices mapping\"\n                    shard_offset, shard_size = module.fused_weight_shard_indices_mapping[\n                        shard_key]\n                    copy_weight_shard(module.weight, weight, shard_offset,\n                                      shard_size)\n\n    def pre_reload_weights(self, module: Linear):\n        for param_name, metadata in module.rebuild_tensor_metadata.items():\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"Pre-reloading weight '{param_name}' requires tensor re-creation, which will invalidate existing CUDA graphs.\"\n            )\n            param = Parameter(torch.empty_like(metadata, device=\"cuda\"),\n                              requires_grad=False)\n            module.register_parameter(param_name, param)\n\n\nclass FP8QDQLinearMethod(UnquantizedLinearMethod):\n\n    def create_weights(self, module: Linear, in_features: int,\n                       out_features: int, bias: bool, dtype: torch.dtype):\n        weight_shape = (out_features, in_features)\n        module.weight = Parameter(torch.empty(weight_shape,\n                                              dtype=torch.float8_e4m3fn),\n                                  requires_grad=False)\n        module.weight_scale = Parameter(torch.tensor(1., dtype=torch.float32),\n                                        requires_grad=False)\n        module.input_scale = Parameter(torch.tensor(1., dtype=torch.float32),\n                                       requires_grad=False)\n        module.inv_input_scale = Parameter(torch.tensor(1.,\n                                                        dtype=torch.float32),\n                                           requires_grad=False)\n        # K, V scales for NVFP4 KV cache\n        module.kv_scales = Parameter(torch.ones(3, dtype=torch.float32),\n                                     requires_grad=False)\n        # K, V scales for NVFP4 KV cache\n        module.inv_kv_scales = Parameter(torch.ones(3, dtype=torch.float32),\n                                         requires_grad=False)\n        if bias:\n            module.bias = Parameter(torch.empty((out_features), dtype=dtype),\n                                    requires_grad=False)\n        else:\n            module.register_parameter(\"bias\", None)\n\n        module.rebuild_tensor_metadata = {}\n\n    def apply(self, module: Linear, input: torch.Tensor,\n              bias: Optional[torch.Tensor]):\n        cur_input_scale = module.input_scale\n        if input.dtype != torch.float8_e4m3fn:\n            if module.input_scale is not None and not module.force_dynamic_quantization:\n                # Static quantization\n                qinput, _ = torch.ops.tensorrt_llm.static_quantize_e4m3_per_tensor(\n                    input, module.input_scale)\n            else:\n                # Dynamic quantization\n                qinput, cur_input_scale = torch.ops.tensorrt_llm.quantize_e4m3_per_tensor(\n                    input)\n                cur_input_scale = cur_input_scale.to(torch.float32)\n\n        else:\n            qinput = input\n\n        # This op does not support bias now.\n        if module.enable_cuda_core and qinput.shape[0] <= 8:\n            # use cuda core for small m dimension\n            output = torch.ops.trtllm.cuda_scaled_mm(\n                qinput,\n                module.weight.t(),\n                scale_a=cur_input_scale,\n                scale_b=module.weight_scale,\n                bias=None,\n                out_dtype=module.dtype or input.dtype,\n            )\n        else:\n            output = torch.ops.trtllm.cublas_scaled_mm(\n                qinput,\n                module.weight.t(),\n                scale_a=cur_input_scale,\n                scale_b=module.weight_scale,\n                bias=None,\n                out_dtype=module.dtype or input.dtype,\n            )\n        if bias is not None:\n            output = output + bias\n        return output\n\n    def load_kv_scales(self, weights: List[Dict]):\n        k_scale, v_scale = [], []\n        for w in weights:\n            if \"k_scale\" in w:\n                k_scale.append(w[\"k_scale\"][...].reshape([]))\n            if \"v_scale\" in w:\n                v_scale.append(w[\"v_scale\"][...].reshape([]))\n        return k_scale, v_scale\n\n    def load_weight_scales(self,\n                           weights: List[Dict],\n                           shard_keys: list[str] = None):\n        input_scales, weight_scales = {}, {}\n        if shard_keys is None:\n            for w in weights:\n                if \"input_scale\" in w:\n                    input_scales[None] = w[\"input_scale\"][...].reshape([])\n                if \"weight_scale\" in w:\n                    weight_scales[None] = w[\"weight_scale\"][...].reshape([])\n        else:\n            for shard_key, w in zip(shard_keys, weights):\n                if \"input_scale\" in w:\n                    input_scales[shard_key] = w[\"input_scale\"][...].reshape([])\n                if \"weight_scale\" in w:\n                    weight_scales[shard_key] = w[\"weight_scale\"][...].reshape(\n                        [])\n        return input_scales, weight_scales\n\n    def load_weights_vanilla(self,\n                             module: Linear,\n                             weights: List[Dict],\n                             allow_partial_loading: bool = False) -> None:\n        super().load_weights_vanilla(\n            module, weights, allow_partial_loading=allow_partial_loading)\n        input_scale, weight_scale = self.load_weight_scales(weights)\n        if input_scale:\n            copy_weight(module.input_scale, input_scale[None])\n            module.inv_input_scale.data = 1.0 / module.input_scale\n            setattr(module, \"has_static_input_scale\", True)\n        if weight_scale:\n            copy_weight(module.weight_scale, weight_scale[None])\n\n    def process_weights_after_loading_vanilla(self, module: Linear):\n        if not hasattr(module, \"has_static_input_scale\"):\n            module.input_scale = None\n            module.inv_input_scale = None\n        else:\n            delattr(module, \"has_static_input_scale\")\n\n    def load_weights_fused_qkv_linear(\n            self,\n            module: Linear,\n            weights: List[Dict],\n            allow_partial_loading: bool = False) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load weights for fused QKV linear layer.\n\n        In partial loading mode, only loads weights and scales to their designated positions.\n        The actual rescaling is deferred to process_weights_after_loading_fused_qkv_linear.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Parent class handles weight loading\n        super().load_weights_fused_qkv_linear(\n            module, weights, allow_partial_loading=allow_partial_loading)\n        weight_mode = module.weights_loading_config.weight_mode\n        if not hasattr(module, \"tmp_input_scales\"):\n            module.tmp_input_scales = torch.empty(\n                weight_mode.int_value,\n                dtype=torch.float32,\n                device=module.input_scale.device)\n        if not hasattr(module, \"tmp_weight_scales\"):\n            module.tmp_weight_scales = torch.empty(\n                weight_mode.int_value,\n                dtype=torch.float32,\n                device=module.weight_scale.device)\n        # Load input_scale and weight_scale to tmp_qkv_input_scales and tmp_qkv_weight_scales\n        # q -> index 0, k -> index 1, v -> index 2\n        input_scales, weight_scales = self.load_weight_scales(\n            weights, shard_keys=weight_mode.shard_keys)\n        shard_key_to_index = weight_mode.shard_key_to_index\n\n        for shard_key, scale in input_scales.items():\n            idx = shard_key_to_index[shard_key]\n            module.tmp_input_scales[idx] = scale\n            setattr(module, \"has_static_input_scale\", True)\n\n        for shard_key, scale in weight_scales.items():\n            idx = shard_key_to_index[shard_key]\n            module.tmp_weight_scales[idx] = scale\n\n        # Load k and v scales, used for NVFP4 KV cache\n        # Store them temporarily for post-processing\n        k_scale, v_scale = self.load_kv_scales(weights)\n        if k_scale:\n            if getattr(module, \"tmp_k_scales\", None) is None:\n                module.tmp_k_scales = []\n            module.tmp_k_scales.extend(k_scale)\n        if v_scale:\n            if getattr(module, \"tmp_v_scales\", None) is None:\n                module.tmp_v_scales = []\n            module.tmp_v_scales.extend(v_scale)\n\n    def rescale_fused_weights(self, module: Linear):\n        \"\"\"\n        Helper function to rescale fused weights.\n\n        This method:\n        1. Computes the max input_scale of all shards(qkv or gate/up) and update input_scale parameter to the max value\n        2. Computes the max weight_scale across all shards(qkv or gate/up)\n        3. Rescales each weight shard: weight * original_scale, then divide by max_scale\n        4. Updates weight_scale parameter to the unified max value\n        \"\"\"\n        weight_mode = module.weights_loading_config.weight_mode\n        shard_key_to_index = weight_mode.shard_key_to_index\n\n        # Handle input_scale\n        if hasattr(module, \"has_static_input_scale\"):\n            # Compute max and replace input_scale with a new parameter\n            max_input_scale = module.tmp_input_scales.max()\n            module.input_scale.data.copy_(max_input_scale)\n            delattr(module, \"has_static_input_scale\")\n        else:\n            module.input_scale = None\n\n        # Compute max weight_scale\n        max_weight_scale = module.tmp_weight_scales.max()\n        module.weight_scale.data.copy_(max_weight_scale)\n\n        # Rescale each weight shard: (weight * original_scale) / max_scale\n        for shard_key in weight_mode.shard_keys:\n            idx = shard_key_to_index[shard_key]\n            original_scale = module.tmp_weight_scales[idx]\n\n            # Get shard position from mapping\n            shard_offset, shard_size = module.fused_weight_shard_indices_mapping[\n                shard_key]\n\n            # Rescale: FP8 -> BF16 -> multiply by original_scale -> divide by max_scale -> FP8\n            weight_shard = module.weight.data[shard_offset:shard_offset +\n                                              shard_size]\n            rescaled_weight = weight_shard.to(module.dtype).mul_(original_scale)\n            rescaled_weight = rescaled_weight.div_(\n                max_weight_scale.to(rescaled_weight.device)).to(\n                    torch.float8_e4m3fn)\n            module.weight.data[shard_offset:shard_offset +\n                               shard_size] = rescaled_weight\n\n        delattr(module, \"tmp_input_scales\")\n        delattr(module, \"tmp_weight_scales\")\n\n    def process_weights_after_loading_fused_qkv_linear(self, module: Linear):\n        \"\"\"\n        Post-process weights after all partial loads are complete.\n        \"\"\"\n        self.rescale_fused_weights(module)\n\n        # Handle kv_scales for NVFP4 KV cache\n        if os.environ.get(\"TRTLLM_LOAD_KV_SCALES\", \"1\") == \"1\":\n            k_scales = getattr(module, \"tmp_k_scales\", [])\n            v_scales = getattr(module, \"tmp_v_scales\", [])\n            if k_scales:\n                assert v_scales, \"k_scale and v_scale must be loaded together\"\n                # The calibrated KV scales are amax / (6 * 448), but the requested KV scales are amax / 448,\n                # to avoid overflow when dequantizing NVFP4 in attention kernels.\n                copy_weight(\n                    module.kv_scales,\n                    torch.tensor(\n                        [1.0, max(k_scales).item(),\n                         max(v_scales).item()],\n                        dtype=torch.float32))\n                module.inv_kv_scales.data = 1.0 / module.kv_scales\n\n        # Clean up temporary attributes\n        if hasattr(module, \"tmp_k_scales\"):\n            delattr(module, \"tmp_k_scales\")\n        if hasattr(module, \"tmp_v_scales\"):\n            delattr(module, \"tmp_v_scales\")\n\n    def load_weights_fused_gate_up_linear(\n            self,\n            module: Linear,\n            weights: List[Dict],\n            allow_partial_loading: bool = False) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load weights for fused gate/up linear layer.\n\n        In partial loading mode, only loads weights and scales to their designated positions.\n        The actual rescaling is deferred to process_weights_after_loading_fused_gate_up_linear.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Parent class handles weight loading\n        super().load_weights_fused_gate_up_linear(\n            module, weights, allow_partial_loading=allow_partial_loading)\n        weight_mode = module.weights_loading_config.weight_mode\n        if not hasattr(module, \"tmp_input_scales\"):\n            module.tmp_input_scales = torch.empty(\n                weight_mode.int_value,\n                dtype=torch.float32,\n                device=module.input_scale.device)\n        if not hasattr(module, \"tmp_weight_scales\"):\n            module.tmp_weight_scales = torch.empty(\n                weight_mode.int_value,\n                dtype=torch.float32,\n                device=module.weight_scale.device)\n        # Load input_scale and weight_scale to their designated positions\n        # gate -> index 0, up -> index 1\n        input_scales, weight_scales = self.load_weight_scales(\n            weights, shard_keys=weight_mode.shard_keys)\n        shard_key_to_index = weight_mode.shard_key_to_index\n\n        for shard_key, scale in input_scales.items():\n            idx = shard_key_to_index[shard_key]\n            module.tmp_input_scales[idx] = scale\n            setattr(module, \"has_static_input_scale\", True)\n\n        for shard_key, scale in weight_scales.items():\n            idx = shard_key_to_index[shard_key]\n            module.tmp_weight_scales[idx] = scale\n\n    def process_weights_after_loading_fused_gate_up_linear(\n            self, module: Linear):\n        \"\"\"\n        Post-process weights after all partial loads are complete.\n        \"\"\"\n        self.rescale_fused_weights(module)\n\n\nclass FP8RowwiseLinearMethod(UnquantizedLinearMethod):\n\n    def create_weights(self, module: Linear, in_features: int,\n                       out_features: int, bias: bool, dtype: torch.dtype):\n        weight_shape = (out_features, in_features)\n\n        module.weight = Parameter(torch.empty(weight_shape,\n                                              dtype=torch.float8_e4m3fn),\n                                  requires_grad=False)\n        module.weight_scale = Parameter(torch.empty(out_features),\n                                        requires_grad=False)\n        # Not really used for Gemm now.\n        # Only used to quantize output of FP8 attention.\n        module.input_scale = Parameter(torch.tensor(1., dtype=torch.float32),\n                                       requires_grad=False)\n        module.inv_input_scale = Parameter(torch.tensor(1.,\n                                                        dtype=torch.float32),\n                                           requires_grad=False)\n        if bias:\n            module.bias = Parameter(torch.empty((out_features), dtype=dtype),\n                                    requires_grad=False)\n        else:\n            module.register_parameter(\"bias\", None)\n\n        module.rebuild_tensor_metadata = {}\n\n    def apply(self, module: Linear, input: torch.Tensor,\n              bias: Optional[torch.Tensor]):\n        # FP8 tensor inputs are from attention. Directly use ones as scale.\n        if input.dtype == torch.float8_e4m3fn:\n            qinput = input\n            cur_input_scale = torch.ones(input.shape[0],\n                                         device=input.device,\n                                         dtype=torch.float32)\n        else:\n            # Use dynamic per-token quantization for activation\n            qinput, cur_input_scale = torch.ops.tensorrt_llm.quantize_e4m3_activation(\n                input)\n\n        # This op does not support bias now.\n        output = torch.ops.trtllm.fp8_rowwise_gemm(\n            qinput,\n            module.weight,\n            cur_input_scale.float(),\n            module.weight_scale,\n            module.dtype or input.dtype,\n        )\n        if bias is not None:\n            output = output + bias\n        return output\n\n    def _get_scale_name(self, weights: List[Dict]):\n        # `weight_scale_inv` for DS recipe and  `weight_scale` for ModelOpt recipe.\n        # Actually they hold identical values of data_amax / 448.\n        scale_name = \"weight_scale_inv\"\n        if scale_name not in weights[0]:\n            scale_name = \"weight_scale\"\n        return scale_name\n\n    def load_weights_vanilla(self,\n                             module: Linear,\n                             weights: List[Dict],\n                             allow_partial_loading: bool = False):\n        super().load_weights_vanilla(\n            module, weights, allow_partial_loading=allow_partial_loading)\n        scale_name = self._get_scale_name(weights)\n        if scale_name in weights[0]:\n            weight_scale = load_weight_shard(weights[0][scale_name],\n                                             module.tp_size, module.tp_rank,\n                                             module.tp_mode)\n            copy_weight(module.weight_scale, weight_scale)\n        if \"input_scale\" in weights[0]:\n            copy_weight(module.input_scale, weights[0][\"input_scale\"])\n            module.inv_input_scale.data = 1.0 / module.input_scale\n\n    def load_weights_fused_qkv_linear(self,\n                                      module: Linear,\n                                      weights: List[Dict],\n                                      allow_partial_loading: bool = False):\n        super().load_weights_fused_qkv_linear(\n            module, weights, allow_partial_loading=allow_partial_loading)\n        scale_name = self._get_scale_name(weights)\n        q_scale = load_weight_shard(\n            weights[0][scale_name], module.tp_size, module.tp_rank,\n            module.tp_mode) if scale_name in weights[0] else None\n        k_scale = load_weight_shard(\n            weights[1][scale_name], module.tp_size, module.tp_rank,\n            module.tp_mode) if scale_name in weights[1] else None\n        v_scale = load_weight_shard(\n            weights[2][scale_name], module.tp_size, module.tp_rank,\n            module.tp_mode) if scale_name in weights[2] else None\n        for shard_key, scale in zip(\n                module.fused_weight_shard_indices_mapping.keys(),\n            [q_scale, k_scale, v_scale]):\n            if scale is not None:\n                shard_offset, shard_size = module.fused_weight_shard_indices_mapping[\n                    shard_key]\n                copy_weight_shard(module.weight_scale, scale, shard_offset,\n                                  shard_size)\n\n    def load_weights_fused_gate_up_linear(\n            self,\n            module: Linear,\n            weights: List[Dict],\n            allow_partial_loading: bool = False) -> None:\n        super().load_weights_fused_gate_up_linear(\n            module, weights, allow_partial_loading=allow_partial_loading)\n        scale_name = self._get_scale_name(weights)\n        gate_scale = load_weight_shard(\n            weights[0][scale_name], module.tp_size, module.tp_rank,\n            module.tp_mode) if scale_name in weights[0] else None\n        up_scale = load_weight_shard(\n            weights[1][scale_name], module.tp_size, module.tp_rank,\n            module.tp_mode) if scale_name in weights[1] else None\n        for shard_key, scale in zip(\n                module.fused_weight_shard_indices_mapping.keys(),\n            [gate_scale, up_scale]):\n            if scale is not None:\n                shard_offset, shard_size = module.fused_weight_shard_indices_mapping[\n                    shard_key]\n                copy_weight_shard(module.weight_scale, scale, shard_offset,\n                                  shard_size)\n\n\nclass FP8BlockScalesLinearMethod(UnquantizedLinearMethod):\n\n    def create_weights(self, module: Linear, in_features: int,\n                       out_features: int, bias: bool, dtype: torch.dtype):\n        weight_shape = (out_features, in_features)\n\n        module.weight = Parameter(torch.empty(weight_shape,\n                                              dtype=torch.float8_e4m3fn),\n                                  requires_grad=False)\n        scale_shape = (math.ceil(out_features / 128),\n                       math.ceil(in_features / 128))\n        module.weight_scale = Parameter(torch.empty(scale_shape,\n                                                    dtype=torch.float32),\n                                        requires_grad=False)\n        # Not really used for Gemm now.\n        # Only used to quantize output of FP8 attention.\n        module.input_scale = Parameter(torch.tensor(1., dtype=torch.float32),\n                                       requires_grad=False)\n        module.inv_input_scale = Parameter(torch.tensor(1.,\n                                                        dtype=torch.float32),\n                                           requires_grad=False)\n        if bias:\n            module.bias = Parameter(torch.empty((out_features), dtype=dtype),\n                                    requires_grad=False)\n        else:\n            module.register_parameter(\"bias\", None)\n\n        module.rebuild_tensor_metadata = {}\n\n    def apply(self, module: Linear, input: torch.Tensor,\n              bias: Optional[torch.Tensor]):\n        if input.dtype == torch.float8_e4m3fn:\n            input = input.to(torch.bfloat16) * module.input_scale\n        assert input.dtype == torch.bfloat16\n\n        if is_sm_100f():\n            if module.use_cute_dsl_blockscaling_mm or module.disable_deep_gemm:\n                act_input_fp8, act_input_sf = torch.ops.trtllm.fp8_quantize_1x128(\n                    input)\n                output = torch.ops.trtllm.cute_dsl_fp8_gemm_blackwell(\n                    act_input_fp8, module.weight, act_input_sf,\n                    module.weight_scale)\n            else:\n                output = torch.ops.trtllm.fp8_swap_ab_gemm(\n                    input,\n                    module.weight,\n                    module.weight_scale,\n                    disable_ue8m0_cast=True,\n                )\n        elif get_sm_version() == 120:\n            act_input_fp8, act_input_sf = per_token_quant_and_transform(input)\n            output = torch.ops.trtllm.fp8_block_scaling_gemm(\n                act_input_fp8, module.weight, act_input_sf, module.weight_scale)\n        else:\n            act_input_fp8, act_input_sf = torch.ops.trtllm.fp8_quantize_1x128(\n                input)\n            output = torch.ops.trtllm.fp8_block_scaling_gemm(\n                act_input_fp8, module.weight, act_input_sf, module.weight_scale)\n\n        if bias is not None:\n            output = output + bias\n        return output\n\n    def _get_scale_name(self, weights: List[Dict]):\n        # `weight_scale_inv` for DS recipe and  `weight_scale` for ModelOpt recipe.\n        # Actually they hold identical values of data_amax / 448.\n        for w in weights:\n            if \"weight_scale_inv\" in w:\n                return \"weight_scale_inv\"\n        return \"weight_scale\"\n\n    def load_weights_vanilla(self,\n                             module: Linear,\n                             weights: List[Dict],\n                             allow_partial_loading: bool = False) -> None:\n        super().load_weights_vanilla(\n            module, weights, allow_partial_loading=allow_partial_loading)\n\n        scale_name = self._get_scale_name(weights)\n        if scale_name in weights[0]:\n            full_weight_scale = weights[0][scale_name]\n            # modelopt fp8_pb_wo can have 2 extra singleton dimensions\n            if full_weight_scale.dim() == 4:\n                full_weight_scale = full_weight_scale.squeeze(1).squeeze(-1)\n            weight_scale = load_weight_shard(full_weight_scale, module.tp_size,\n                                             module.tp_rank, module.tp_mode)\n            copy_weight(module.weight_scale, weight_scale)\n        if \"input_scale\" in weights[0]:\n            copy_weight(module.input_scale, weights[0][\"input_scale\"])\n            module.inv_input_scale.data = 1.0 / module.input_scale\n\n    def remap_fused_shard_indices_by_divisible_factor(self, mapping: Dict,\n                                                      divisible_factor: int):\n        \"\"\"\n        Remap fused weight shard indices to scale coordinates by dividing by divisible_factor.\n\n        Args:\n            mapping: Dict of {shard_key: (offset, size)} in weight coordinates\n            divisible_factor: Block size (e.g., 128 for block-scale quantization)\n\n        Returns:\n            Dict of {shard_key: (scale_offset, scale_size)} in scale coordinates\n        \"\"\"\n        result = {}\n        for key, (offset, size) in mapping.items():\n            scale_offset = math.ceil(offset / divisible_factor)\n            scale_size = math.ceil(size / divisible_factor)\n            result[key] = (scale_offset, scale_size)\n        return result\n\n    def load_weights_fused_qkv_linear(\n            self,\n            module: Linear,\n            weights: List[Dict],\n            allow_partial_loading: bool = False) -> None:\n        super().load_weights_fused_qkv_linear(\n            module, weights, allow_partial_loading=allow_partial_loading)\n\n        scale_name = self._get_scale_name(weights)\n        # modelopt fp8_pb_wo can have 2 extra singleton dimensions\n        full_scales = [\n            w[scale_name] if scale_name in w else None for w in weights[:3]\n        ]\n        full_scales_squeezed = [\n            s.squeeze(1).squeeze(-1) if s is not None and s.dim() == 4 else s\n            for s in full_scales\n        ]\n\n        scales = [\n            load_weight_shard(s, module.tp_size, module.tp_rank, module.tp_mode)\n            if s is not None else None for s in full_scales_squeezed\n        ]\n        processed_mapping = self.remap_fused_shard_indices_by_divisible_factor(\n            module.fused_weight_shard_indices_mapping, 128)\n        for shard_key, scale in zip(processed_mapping.keys(), scales):\n            if scale is not None:\n                shard_offset, shard_size = processed_mapping[shard_key]\n                copy_weight_shard(module.weight_scale, scale, shard_offset,\n                                  shard_size)\n\n    def load_weights_fused_gate_up_linear(\n            self,\n            module: Linear,\n            weights: List[Dict],\n            allow_partial_loading: bool = False) -> None:\n        super().load_weights_fused_gate_up_linear(\n            module, weights, allow_partial_loading=allow_partial_loading)\n\n        scale_name = self._get_scale_name(weights)\n        full_scales = [\n            w[scale_name] if scale_name in w else None for w in weights[:2]\n        ]\n        full_scales_squeezed = [\n            s.squeeze(1).squeeze(-1) if s is not None and s.dim() == 4 else s\n            for s in full_scales\n        ]\n        scales = [\n            load_weight_shard(s, module.tp_size, module.tp_rank, module.tp_mode)\n            if s is not None else None for s in full_scales_squeezed\n        ]\n        processed_mapping = self.remap_fused_shard_indices_by_divisible_factor(\n            module.fused_weight_shard_indices_mapping, 128)\n        for shard_key, scale in zip(processed_mapping.keys(), scales):\n            if scale is not None:\n                shard_offset, shard_size = processed_mapping[shard_key]\n                copy_weight_shard(module.weight_scale, scale, shard_offset,\n                                  shard_size)\n\n    def post_load_weights(self, module: Linear):\n        super().post_load_weights(module)\n        if (is_sm_100f() and not (module.use_cute_dsl_blockscaling_mm\n                                 or module.disable_deep_gemm)) or \\\n           get_sm_version() == 120:\n            weight, weight_scale = resmooth_to_fp8_e8m0(module.weight,\n                                                        module.weight_scale)\n            transformed_scale = transform_sf_into_required_layout(\n                weight_scale,\n                mn=weight.shape[0],\n                k=weight.shape[1],\n                recipe=(1, 128, 128),\n                is_sfa=False)\n            replace_parameter_and_save_metadata(\n                module, \"weight\", nn.Parameter(weight, requires_grad=False),\n                module.rebuild_tensor_metadata)\n            replace_parameter_and_save_metadata(\n                module, \"weight_scale\",\n                nn.Parameter(transformed_scale, requires_grad=False),\n                module.rebuild_tensor_metadata)\n\n\nclass NVFP4LinearMethod(LinearMethodBase):\n\n    def create_weights(self, module: Linear, in_features: int,\n                       out_features: int, bias: bool, dtype: torch.dtype):\n        module.scaling_vector_size = 16\n        assert in_features % module.scaling_vector_size == 0, f\"in_features {in_features} must be divisible by scaling_vector_size {module.scaling_vector_size}\"\n\n        # Quantized weights\n        module.weight = Parameter(torch.empty([out_features, in_features // 2],\n                                              dtype=fp4_utils.float4_e2m1x2),\n                                  requires_grad=False)\n\n        # FP8 per-block scaling factors. dtype must be aligned with SF_DTYPE\n        # Padding is required. See computeSFSize in quantization.h\n        nrows = fp4_utils.pad_up(out_features, 128)\n        ncols = fp4_utils.pad_up(in_features // module.scaling_vector_size, 4)\n        module.weight_scale = Parameter(torch.empty(\n            [nrows * ncols], dtype=fp4_utils.float4_sf_dtype),\n                                        requires_grad=False)\n\n        # FP32 per-tensor global scaling factor = 448*6/amax_input\n        module.input_scale = Parameter(torch.empty([1], dtype=torch.float32),\n                                       requires_grad=False)\n        module.inv_input_scale = Parameter(torch.empty([1],\n                                                       dtype=torch.float32),\n                                           requires_grad=False)\n\n        # (amax_input * amax_weight) / (448*6 * 448*6)\n        module.alpha = Parameter(torch.empty([1], dtype=torch.float32),\n                                 requires_grad=False)\n\n        # K, V scales for NVFP4 KV cache\n        module.kv_scales = Parameter(torch.ones(3, dtype=torch.float32),\n                                     requires_grad=False)\n        # K, V scales for NVFP4 KV cache\n        module.inv_kv_scales = Parameter(torch.ones(3, dtype=torch.float32),\n                                         requires_grad=False)\n\n        # NOTE: Not in all linear we have this tensor - pre_quant_scale is computed as an average and merged with the\n        # LayerNorm for QKV and Gate/Up projection layers when possible. we can see the tensor only for o_proj and down_proj\n        module.pre_quant_scale = None\n\n        if bias:\n            module.bias = Parameter(torch.empty((out_features), dtype=dtype),\n                                    requires_grad=False)\n        else:\n            module.register_parameter(\"bias\", None)\n\n    def _input_prepare(self, module: Linear, input: torch.Tensor):\n        if isinstance(input, Fp4QuantizedTensor):\n            # Input is already quantized - this should not happen if pre_quant_scale exists\n            # because we disable FP4 output for attention output when pre_quant_scale is present\n            if module.pre_quant_scale is not None:\n                raise RuntimeError(\n                    \"Received FP4 quantized input but pre_quant_scale exists. \"\n                    \"This indicates FP4 output was not properly disabled for the previous layer.\"\n                )\n            act_fp4, act_sf = input.fp4_tensor, input.scaling_factor\n        elif isinstance(input, tuple):\n            # Input is a tuple of (fp4_tensor, scaling_factor)\n            if module.pre_quant_scale is not None:\n                raise RuntimeError(\n                    \"Received FP4 quantized tuple input but pre_quant_scale exists. \"\n                    \"This indicates FP4 output was not properly disabled for the previous layer.\"\n                )\n            act_fp4, act_sf = input\n        else:\n            # Input is a regular tensor () - apply pre_quant_scale if it exists (for NVFP4_AWQ)\n            if module.pre_quant_scale is not None:\n                assert input.dtype == module.pre_quant_scale.dtype, \"Input dtype and pre_quant_scale dtype must match\"\n                input = input * module.pre_quant_scale\n\n            act_fp4, act_sf = torch.ops.trtllm.fp4_quantize(\n                input, module.input_scale, module.scaling_vector_size, False)\n        return act_fp4, act_sf\n\n    def apply(self, module: Linear, input: torch.Tensor,\n              bias: Optional[torch.Tensor]):\n        act_fp4, act_sf = self._input_prepare(module, input)\n        # Use unified interface - supports CUTLASS, cuBLASLt, CuteDSL\n        # Convert list to comma-separated string for torch.compile compatibility\n        allowed_backends_str = ','.join(module.nvfp4_allowed_backends)\n        output = torch.ops.trtllm.nvfp4_gemm(\n            act_fp4,\n            module.weight,\n            act_sf,\n            module.weight_scale,\n            module.alpha,\n            module.dtype,\n            to_userbuffers=False,\n            allowed_backends=allowed_backends_str)\n        # Take the dim of out_features if padded. Make sure the output is contiguous\n        if output.shape[-1] > module.out_features:\n            output = output[..., :module.out_features].contiguous()\n\n        if bias is not None:\n            output = output + bias\n        return output\n\n    def apply_linear_allreduce(self, module: Linear, input: torch.Tensor,\n                               bias: Optional[torch.Tensor], tp_rank: int,\n                               tp_group: List[int]):\n        act_fp4, act_sf = self._input_prepare(module, input)\n        output = torch.ops.trtllm.nvfp4_gemm_allreduce(\n            act_fp4, module.weight, act_sf, module.weight_scale, module.alpha,\n            module.dtype, tp_rank, tp_group)\n        # Take the dim of out_features if padded. Make sure the output is contiguous\n        if output.shape[-1] > module.out_features:\n            output = output[..., :module.out_features].contiguous()\n\n        if bias is not None:\n            output = output + bias\n        return output\n\n    def load_kv_scales(self, weights: List[Dict]):\n        k_scale, v_scale = [], []\n        for w in weights:\n            if \"k_scale\" in w:\n                k_scale.append(w[\"k_scale\"][...].reshape([]))\n            if \"v_scale\" in w:\n                v_scale.append(w[\"v_scale\"][...].reshape([]))\n        return k_scale, v_scale\n\n    def load_weight_scales(self,\n                           weights: List[Dict],\n                           tp_size: int = 1,\n                           tp_rank: int = 0,\n                           tp_mode: Optional[TensorParallelMode] = None):\n        # For concatenated weights (qkv_proj / up_gate_proj), the global scaling factors and input scaling factors should be shared.\n        input_scale = None\n        weight_scale_2 = None\n        weight_scale = []\n\n        device = torch.device(\"cuda\")\n\n        for w in weights:\n            if \"input_scale\" in w:\n                if input_scale is None:\n                    input_scale = w[\"input_scale\"][...]\n                else:\n                    assert input_scale == w[\"input_scale\"][\n                        ...], \"The input_scale should be same for all the weights\"\n            if \"weight_scale\" in w:\n                ws = load_weight_shard(w[\"weight_scale\"],\n                                       tp_size,\n                                       tp_rank,\n                                       tp_mode,\n                                       device=device).contiguous()\n                assert ws.dtype == torch.float8_e4m3fn  #
